571: fn test_pipeline(pass: &mut u32, fail: &mut u32) {
572:     let ws = TestWorkspace::new("pipeline");
573: 
574:     // Multi-Phase Social Media Content Pipeline
575:     // Each phase is a Shell step, ordered by dependencies.
576:     // This tests real automation composition: integrated end-to-end flow.
577:     let pipeline = vec![
578:         // Phase 1: Research — discover trending topics
579:         shell_step(
580:             "phase1_research",
581:             "echo 'RESEARCH: Found 3 trending topics [AI, Rust, WebAssembly]'",
582:             vec![],
583:         ),
584:         // Phase 2: Create — generate content drafts
585:         shell_step(
586:             "phase2_create",
587:             "echo 'CREATE: Generated draft for topic 1' && echo 'CREATE: Generated draft for topic 2'",
588:             vec!["phase1_research"],
589:         ),
590:         // Phase 3: Review — quality check
591:         shell_step(
592:             "phase3_review",
593:             "echo 'REVIEW: All drafts passed quality check (score=8.5/10)'",
594:             vec!["phase2_create"],
595:         ),
596:         // Phase 4: Publish — deploy to platforms
597:         shell_step(
598:             "phase4_publish",
599:             "echo 'PUBLISH: Published to [Twitter, LinkedIn, Blog]'",
600:             vec!["phase3_review"],
601:         ),
602:         // Phase 5: Report — summarize results
603:         shell_step(
604:             "phase5_report",
605:             "echo 'REPORT: Pipeline complete — 3 topics → 2 drafts → reviewed → published'",
606:             vec!["phase4_publish"],
607:         ),
608:     ];
609: 
610:     let result = run_async(FlowEngine::execute(&pipeline, None, ws.rt(), ws.cache()));
611:     match result {
612:         Ok(outputs) => {
613:             let expected = ["phase1_research", "phase2_create", "phase3_review", "phase4_publish", "phase5_report"];
614:             let ids: Vec<&str> = outputs.iter().map(|(id, _)| id.as_str()).collect();
615: 
616:             // Check all expected steps present
617:             let all_present: bool = expected.iter().all(|e| ids.contains(e));
618:             if !all_present {
619:                 *fail += 1;
620:                 println!("  ❌ Pipeline: missing some steps. Got: {ids:?}");
621:                 return;
622:             }
623: 
624:             // Check ordering (each phase after its dependency)
625:             let pos1 = ids.iter().position(|&id| id == "phase1_research").unwrap();
626:             let pos2 = ids.iter().position(|&id| id == "phase2_create").unwrap();
627:             let pos3 = ids.iter().position(|&id| id == "phase3_review").unwrap();
628:             let pos4 = ids.iter().position(|&id| id == "phase4_publish").unwrap();
629:             let pos5 = ids.iter().position(|&id| id == "phase5_report").unwrap();
630: 
631:             if pos1 < pos2 && pos2 < pos3 && pos3 < pos4 && pos4 < pos5 {
632:                 *pass += 1;
633:                 println!("  ✅ Pipeline: all 5 phases executed in correct order");
634: 
635:                 // Check output content
636:                 if let Some((_, output)) = outputs.iter().find(|(id, _)| id == "phase1_research") {
637:                     let stdout = output.get("stdout").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("");
638:                     if stdout.contains("RESEARCH:") {
639:                         *pass += 1;
640:                         println!("  ✅ Pipeline: phase1 output verified");
641:                     } else {
642:                         *fail += 1;
643:                         println!("  ❌ Pipeline: phase1 unexpected output: {stdout:?}");
644:                     }
645:                 }
646:                 if let Some((_, output)) = outputs.iter().find(|(id, _)| id == "phase4_publish") {
647:                     let stdout = output.get("stdout").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("");
648:                     if stdout.contains("PUBLISH:") {
649:                         *pass += 1;
650:                         println!("  ✅ Pipeline: phase4 output verified");
651:                     } else {
652:                         *fail += 1;
653:                         println!("  ❌ Pipeline: phase4 unexpected output: {stdout:?}");
654:                     }
655:                 }
656:             } else {
657:                 *fail += 1;
658:                 println!("  ❌ Pipeline: wrong order {pos1}→{pos2}→{pos3}→{pos4}→{pos5}");
659:             }
660:         }
661:         Err(e) => {
662:             *fail += 1;
663:             println!("  ❌ Pipeline execution failed: {e}");
664:         }
665:     }
666: 
667:     // ── Pipeline Graph Interaction ──
668:     // After pipeline execution, log results into the knowledge graph
669:     let graph_result = test_pipeline_graph_output(ws.cache());
670:     match graph_result {
671:         Ok(node_count) => {
672:             if node_count > 0 {
673:                 *pass += 1;
674:                 println!("  ✅ Pipeline→Graph: created {node_count} nodes from pipeline output");
675:             } else {
676:                 *fail += 1;
677:                 println!("  ❌ Pipeline→Graph: no nodes created");
678:             }
679:         }
680:         Err(e) => {
681:             *fail += 1;
682:             println!("  ❌ Pipeline→Graph: {e}");
683:         }
684:     }
685: }

687: /// Creates graph nodes simulating how pipeline output flows into the knowledge graph
688: fn test_pipeline_graph_output(cache_dir: &std::path::Path) -> StdResult<usize, String> {
689:     let graph_dir = cache_dir.join("graph_test");
690:     std::fs::create_dir_all(&graph_dir).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
691:     let store = GraphStore::open(&graph_dir).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
692: 
693:     // Create nodes for each pipeline phase result
694:     let phases = vec![
695:         ("phase1_research", "Research trending topics"),
696:         ("phase2_create", "Create content drafts"),
697:         ("phase3_review", "Review quality"),
698:         ("phase4_publish", "Publish to platforms"),
699:         ("phase5_report", "Pipeline report"),
700:     ];
701: 
702:     let mut ids = Vec::new();
703:     for (phase_id, summary) in &phases {
704:         let mut props = HashMap::new();
705:         props.insert("summary".to_string(), serde_json::Value::String(summary.to_string()));
706:         props.insert("phase".to_string(), serde_json::Value::String(phase_id.to_string()));
707:         props.insert("timestamp".to_string(), serde_json::Value::String(
708:             std::time::SystemTime::now()
709:                 .duration_since(std::time::UNIX_EPOCH)
710:                 .map(|d| d.as_secs().to_string())
711:                 .unwrap_or_default()
712:         ));
713: 
714:         let id = store.add_node(
715:             NodeKind::Artifact,
716:             phase_id,
717:             props,
718:         ).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
719:         ids.push(id);
720:     }
721: 
722:     // Link phases sequentially: research → create → review → publish → report
723:     for i in 0..ids.len() - 1 {
724:         store.add_edge(
725:             &ids[i],
726:             &ids[i + 1],
727:             EdgeKind::DependsOn,
728:             HashMap::new(),
729:         ).map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
730:     }
731: 
732:     // Verify
733:     let nodes = store.all_nodes().map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
734:     let edges = store.all_edges().map_err(|e| e.to_string())?;
735: 
736:     if nodes.len() != 5 || edges.len() != 4 {
737:         return Err(format!("Expected 5 nodes + 4 edges, got {} nodes + {} edges", nodes.len(), edges.len()));
738:     }
739: 
740:     Ok(nodes.len())
741: }

747: fn test_graph_create_and_query(pass: &mut u32, fail: &mut u32) {

757:             let id = match store.add_node(NodeKind::Artifact, "test-node", props) {
758:                 Ok(id) => id,
759:                 Err(e) => {

775:                     return;
776:                 }
777:             };
778:             let found = nodes.iter().any(|n| n.id == id && n.name == "test-node");

781:             // Test find by kind

783:                 Ok(n) => n,
784:                 Err(e) => {
785:                     *fail += 1;

787:                     let _ = std::fs::remove_dir_all(&dir);
788:                     return;
789:                 }
790:             };

794:             *fail += 1;
795:             println!("  ❌ Graph: open failed: {e}");

798:     let _ = std::fs::remove_dir_all(&dir);

801: fn test_graph_pathfind(pass: &mut u32, fail: &mut u32) {
802:     let dir = std::env::temp_dir().join(format!("automaton-graph-pf-{}", std::process::id()));
803:     let _ = std::fs::remove_dir_all(&dir);
804:     std::fs::create_dir_all(&dir).unwrap();
805: 
806:     let store = GraphStore::open(&dir);
807:     match store {
808:         Ok(store) => {
809:             // Create a simple graph: A → B → C
810:             let a = store.add_node(NodeKind::Artifact, "A", HashMap::new()).unwrap();
811:             let b = store.add_node(NodeKind::Artifact, "B", HashMap::new()).unwrap();
812:             let c = store.add_node(NodeKind::Artifact, "C", HashMap::new()).unwrap();
813: 
814:             store.add_edge(&a, &b, EdgeKind::DependsOn, HashMap::new()).unwrap();
815:             store.add_edge(&b, &c, EdgeKind::DependsOn, HashMap::new()).unwrap();
816: 
817:             // Find path from A to C
818:             match store.find_path(&a, &c) {
819:                 Ok(paths) => {
820:                     if !paths.is_empty() {
821:                         let path = &paths[0];
822:                         if path.len() == 3 { // A → B → C = 3 nodes
823:                             *pass += 1;
824:                             println!("  ✅ Graph pathfind: found path A→B→C ({} nodes)", path.len());
825:                         } else {
826:                             *fail += 1;
827:                             println!("  ❌ Graph pathfind: expected 3 nodes, got {}", path.len());
828:                         }
829:                     } else {
830:                         *fail += 1;
831:                         println!("  ❌ Graph pathfind: no path found");
832:                     }
833:                 }
834:                 Err(e) => {
835:                     *fail += 1;
836:                     println!("  ❌ Graph pathfind: {e}");
837:                 }
838:             }
839:         }
840:         Err(e) => {
841:             *fail += 1;
842:             println!("  ❌ Graph pathfind: open failed: {e}");
843:         }
844:     }
845:     let _ = std::fs::remove_dir_all(&dir);
846: }
847: 
848: fn test_graph_summarize(pass: &mut u32, fail: &mut u32) {
849:     let dir = std::env::temp_dir().join(format!("automaton-graph-ss-{}", std::process::id()));
850:     let _ = std::fs::remove_dir_all(&dir);
851:     std::fs::create_dir_all(&dir).unwrap();
852: 
853:     let store = GraphStore::open(&dir);
854:     match store {
855:         Ok(store) => {
856:             // Create nodes of different kinds
857:             store.add_node(NodeKind::Artifact, "art1", HashMap::new()).unwrap();
858:             store.add_node(NodeKind::Artifact, "art2", HashMap::new()).unwrap();
859:             store.add_node(NodeKind::Module, "mod1", HashMap::new()).unwrap();
860:             store.add_node(NodeKind::Workflow, "wf1", HashMap::new()).unwrap();
861: 
862:             // Create edges
863:             let nodes = store.all_nodes().unwrap();
864:             if nodes.len() >= 2 {
865:                 let _ = store.add_edge(&nodes[0].id, &nodes[1].id, EdgeKind::DependsOn, HashMap::new());
866:             }
867: 
868:             match store.summarize() {
869:                 Ok(summary) => {
870:                     if summary.total_nodes == 4 && summary.total_edges == 1 {
871:                         *pass += 1;
872:                         println!("  ✅ Graph summarize: 4 nodes, 1 edge");
873:                     } else {
874:                         *fail += 1;
875:                         println!("  ❌ Graph summarize: expected 4n+1e, got {}n+{}e", summary.total_nodes, summary.total_edges);
876:                     }
877: 
878:                     let has_artifact = summary.nodes_by_kind.get("Artifact").copied().unwrap_or(0) == 2;
879:                     let has_module = summary.nodes_by_kind.get("Module").copied().unwrap_or(0) == 1;
880:                     let has_workflow = summary.nodes_by_kind.get("Workflow").copied().unwrap_or(0) == 1;
881:                     if has_artifact && has_module && has_workflow {
882:                         *pass += 1;
883:                         println!("  ✅ Graph summarize: breakdown by kind correct");
884:                     } else {
885:                         *fail += 1;
886:                         println!("  ❌ Graph summarize: unexpected kind breakdown: {:?}", summary.nodes_by_kind);
887:                     }
888:                 }
889:                 Err(e) => {
890:                     *fail += 1;
891:                     println!("  ❌ Graph summarize: {e}");
892:                 }
893:             }
894:         }
895:         Err(e) => {
896:             *fail += 1;
897:             println!("  ❌ Graph summarize: open failed: {e}");
898:         }
899:     }
900:     let _ = std::fs::remove_dir_all(&dir);
901: }

905: fn test_ok<F>(pass: &mut u32, fail: &mut u32, name: &str, f: F)
906: where
907:     F: FnOnce() -> StdResult<(), String>,
908: {
909:     match f() {

911:             *pass += 1;

913:         }

922: where
923:     F: FnOnce() -> StdResult<(), String>,
924: {

929:         }

1: pub mod flow;
2: 
3: use std::collections::{BTreeMap, HashMap};
4: use std::sync::Arc;
5: 
6: use automaton_core::backend::RegistryBackend;
7: use automaton_core::*;
8: use automaton_graph::GraphStore;
9: use automaton_runtime::Runtime;
10: use futures::future::join_all;
11: use petgraph::graph::DiGraph;
12: use petgraph::algo::toposort;
13: use petgraph::prelude::*;
14: 
15: /// Options for planning a workflow.
16: #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
17: pub struct PlanOptions {
18:     pub max_depth: usize,
19:     pub include_alternatives: bool,
20:     pub dry_run: bool,
21: }
22: 
23: impl Default for PlanOptions {
24:     fn default() -> Self {
25:         Self { max_depth: 10, include_alternatives: false, dry_run: false }
26:     }
27: }
28: 
29: /// The engine orchestrates planning, materialization, and execution.
30: pub struct Engine {
31:     backend: Arc<dyn RegistryBackend>,
32:     graph: GraphStore,
33:     runtime: Runtime,
34: }
35: 
36: impl Engine {
37:     pub fn new(backend: Arc<dyn RegistryBackend>, graph: GraphStore, runtime: Runtime) -> Self {
38:         Self { backend, graph, runtime }
39:     }
40: 
41:     pub fn backend(&self) -> &Arc<dyn RegistryBackend> {
42:         &self.backend
43:     }
44: 
45:     pub fn registry_deprecated(&self) -> &Arc<dyn RegistryBackend> {
46:         &self.backend
47:     }
48: 
49:     pub fn graph(&self) -> &GraphStore {
50:         &self.graph
51:     }
52: 
53:     /// Plan a workflow: discover the dependency graph from the registry.
54:     pub async fn plan(&self, start_module: &str, options: &PlanOptions) -> Result<RunGraph> {
55:         let has_module = self.backend.get_module(start_module).await?
56:             .ok_or_else(|| AutomatonError::ModuleNotFound(start_module.to_string()))?;
57:         let _ = has_module;
58: 
59:         let run_id = uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string();
60:         let mut modules = vec![];
61:         let mut visited = std::collections::HashSet::new();
62:         let mut stack = vec![(start_module.to_string(), 0)];
63: 
64:         while let Some((path, depth)) = stack.pop() {
65:             if !visited.insert(path.clone()) || depth > options.max_depth {
66:                 continue;
67:             }
68: 
69:             if let Some(mod_data) = self.backend.get_module(&path).await? {
70:                 let deps: Vec<String> = mod_data.manifest.depends_on.iter().map(|d| d.name.clone()).collect();
71: 
72:                 let node = ModuleNode {
73:                     id: format!("{}-{}", path.replace('.', "-"),
74:                         uuid::Uuid::new_v4().to_string().split('-').next().unwrap_or("0")),
75:                     module_id: ModuleId {
76:                         path: path.clone(),
77:                         version: semver::Version::parse(&mod_data.manifest.version)
78:                             .map_err(|e: semver::Error| AutomatonError::Other(e.to_string()))?,
79:                         hash: mod_data.hash.clone(),
80:                         created_at: chrono::Utc::now(),
81:                     },
82:                     input: serde_json::json!({}),
83:                     retry: mod_data.manifest.retry.clone(),
84:                     timeout_ms: mod_data.manifest.timeout_ms,
85:                     depends_on: deps.clone(),
86:                     parallel_group: None,
87:                     condition: None,
88:                     error_handler: None,
89:                 };
90:                 modules.push(node);
91: 
92:                 for dep in &mod_data.manifest.depends_on {
93:                     stack.push((dep.name.clone(), depth + 1));
94:                 }
95:             }
96:         }
97: 
98:         Ok(RunGraph {
99:             id: run_id,
100:             workflow_name: start_module.to_string(),
101:             modules,
102:             steps: vec![],
103:             created_at: chrono::Utc::now(),
104:         })
105:     }
106: 
107:     /// Materialize a run graph into an executable DAG, verifying acyclicity.
108:     pub fn materialize(&self, run_graph: &RunGraph) -> Result<ExecutableDag> {
109:         let mut dag = DiGraph::<ExecNode, ()>::new();
110:         let mut node_indices: HashMap<String, NodeIndex> = HashMap::new();
111: 
112:         for module in &run_graph.modules {
113:             let idx = dag.add_node(ExecNode {
114:                 id: module.id.clone(),
115:                 module_name: module.module_id.path.clone(),
116:                 input: module.input.clone(),
117:                 retry: module.retry.clone(),
118:                 timeout_ms: module.timeout_ms,
119:                 state: ExecutionState::Pending,
120:             });
121:             node_indices.insert(module.id.clone(), idx);
122:         }
123: 
124:         for module in &run_graph.modules {
125:             let target_idx = match node_indices.get(&module.id) {
126:                 Some(idx) => *idx,
127:                 None => continue,
128:             };
129:             for dep in &module.depends_on {
130:                 if let Some(found) = run_graph.modules.iter().find(|m| m.module_id.path == *dep)
131:                     && let Some(source_idx) = node_indices.get(&found.id) {
132:                         dag.add_edge(*source_idx, target_idx, ());
133:                     }
134:             }
135:         }
136: 
137:         match toposort(&dag, None) {
138:             Ok(_) => {}
139:             Err(_) => return Err(AutomatonError::CyclicDependency),
140:         }
141: 
142:         Ok(ExecutableDag { graph: dag, node_indices, run_graph_id: run_graph.id.clone() })
143:     }
144: 
145:     /// Execute a materialized DAG with level-based parallelism.
146:     /// Nodes at the same topological level (no dependency between them) run concurrently.
147:     /// Cross-step state is accumulated in a shared map for downstream reference.
148:     /// Input templates like `${module_path}` or `${module_path.field}` are resolved
149:     /// against upstream results before execution.
150:     pub async fn execute(&self, mut dag: ExecutableDag) -> Result<RunResult> {
151:         let order = toposort(&dag.graph, None)
152:             .map_err(|_| AutomatonError::CyclicDependency)?;
153: 
154:         // Compute topological levels (max depth from root nodes)
155:         let mut levels: HashMap<NodeIndex, usize> = HashMap::new();
156:         for &node_idx in &order {
157:             let lvl = dag.graph.neighbors_directed(node_idx, Incoming)
158:                 .filter_map(|incoming| levels.get(&incoming))
159:                 .max()
160:                 .map(|l| l + 1)
161:                 .unwrap_or(0);
162:             levels.insert(node_idx, lvl);
163:         }
164: 
165:         // Group nodes by level for parallel execution
166:         let mut level_groups: BTreeMap<usize, Vec<NodeIndex>> = BTreeMap::new();
167:         for (&node_idx, &lvl) in &levels {
168:             level_groups.entry(lvl).or_default().push(node_idx);
169:         }
170: 
171:         let run_graph_id = dag.run_graph_id.clone();
172:         // flow_state accumulates outputs keyed by module path for downstream reference
173:         let mut flow_state: HashMap<String, serde_json::Value> = HashMap::new();
174:         let mut states: HashMap<NodeIndex, ExecutionState> = HashMap::new();
175: 
176:         // Initialise all states to Pending
177:         for &node_idx in &order {
178:             states.insert(node_idx, ExecutionState::Pending);
179:         }
180: 
181:         // Execute level by level — all nodes within a level run in parallel
182:         let build_cache_dir = self.backend.build_cache_dir();
183:         for group in level_groups.values() {
184:             // Resolve $var:/$res: references and ${state} references in inputs
185:             let mut resolved_inputs = Vec::with_capacity(group.len());
186:             for &node_idx in group {
187:                 let input = &dag.graph[node_idx].input;
188:                 let with_vars = self.backend.resolve_references(input)
189:                     .await
190:                     .unwrap_or_else(|_| input.clone());
191:                 let resolved = resolve_state_refs(&with_vars, &flow_state);
192:                 resolved_inputs.push(resolved);
193:             }
194: 
195:             let futs: Vec<_> = group.iter().zip(resolved_inputs.iter()).map(|(&node_idx, resolved_input)| {
196:                 let exec_id = dag.graph[node_idx].id.clone();
197:                 let module_path = dag.graph[node_idx].module_name.clone();
198:                 let input = resolved_input.clone();
199:                 let retry = dag.graph[node_idx].retry.clone();
200:                 let timeout = dag.graph[node_idx].timeout_ms;
201:                 let build_cache_dir = build_cache_dir.clone();
202: 
203:                 async move {
204:                     tracing::info!(module = %module_path, "Executing module");
205: 
206:                     let binary_path = build_cache_dir.join(module_path.replace('.', "_"));
207: 
208:                     if binary_path.exists() {
209:                         let result = if let Some(retry_cfg) = &retry {
210:                             self.runtime.run_with_retry(&binary_path, &input, retry_cfg, timeout).await
211:                         } else {
212:                             self.runtime.run_binary(&binary_path, &input, timeout).await
213:                         };
214:                         (node_idx, exec_id, module_path, result)
215:                     } else {
216:                         (node_idx, exec_id, module_path, Err(AutomatonError::Other("No compiled binary".into())))
217:                     }
218:                 }
219:             }).collect();
220: 
221:             let outcomes: Vec<(NodeIndex, String, String, std::result::Result<serde_json::Value, AutomatonError>)> = join_all(futs).await;
222: 
223:             for (node_idx, exec_id, module_path, result) in outcomes {
224:                 match result {
225:                     Ok(output) => {
226:                         tracing::info!(module = %module_path, "Completed");
227:                         let _ = self.backend.record_run(&exec_id, &module_path, &serde_json::json!({})).await;
228:                         let _ = self.backend.update_run(&exec_id, "completed", Some(&output), None, 1).await;
229:                         states.insert(node_idx, ExecutionState::Completed(output.clone()));
230:                         flow_state.insert(module_path, output);
231:                     }
232:                     Err(e) => {
233:                         let err_msg = e.to_string();
234:                         tracing::error!(module = %module_path, error = %err_msg, "Failed");
235:                         let _ = self.backend.record_run(&exec_id, &module_path, &serde_json::json!({})).await;
236:                         let _ = self.backend.update_run(&exec_id, "failed", None, Some(&err_msg), 1).await;
237:                         states.insert(node_idx, ExecutionState::Failed(err_msg.clone()));
238:                         flow_state.insert(module_path, serde_json::json!({"error": err_msg}));
239:                     }
240:                 }
241:             }
242:         }
243: 
244:         // Write final states back to the graph
245:         for (idx, state) in &states {
246:             dag.graph[*idx].state = state.clone();
247:         }
248: 
249:         Ok(RunResult {
250:             run_graph_id,
251:             results: flow_state.clone(),
252:             flow_state: flow_state.clone(),
253:             completed_at: chrono::Utc::now(),
254:         })
255:     }
256: }
257: 
258: /// Resolve state references of the form `${module_path}` or `${module_path.field}`
259: /// in a JSON value, using the accumulated flow_state from completed modules.
260: pub fn resolve_state_refs(val: &serde_json::Value, state: &HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>) -> serde_json::Value {
261:     match val {
262:         serde_json::Value::String(s) => {
263:             if s.starts_with("${") && s.ends_with("}") {
264:                 let inner = &s[2..s.len()-1];
265:                 if let Some(dot_pos) = inner.find('.') {
266:                     let module_part = &inner[..dot_pos];
267:                     let field_part = &inner[dot_pos+1..];
268:                     if let Some(module_output) = state.get(module_part) {
269:                         if let Some(field_val) = module_output.get(field_part) {
270:                             return field_val.clone();
271:                         }
272:                         return serde_json::Value::Null;
273:                     }
274:                     return val.clone();
275:                 }
276:                 // No dot — reference entire module output
277:                 if let Some(module_output) = state.get(inner) {
278:                     return module_output.clone();
279:                 }
280:             }
281:             val.clone()
282:         }
283:         serde_json::Value::Object(map) => {
284:             let mut resolved = serde_json::Map::new();
285:             for (k, v) in map {
286:                 resolved.insert(k.clone(), resolve_state_refs(v, state));
287:             }
288:             serde_json::Value::Object(resolved)
289:         }
290:         serde_json::Value::Array(arr) => {
291:             let resolved: Vec<_> = arr.iter().map(|v| resolve_state_refs(v, state)).collect();
292:             serde_json::Value::Array(resolved)
293:         }
294:         other => other.clone(),
295:     }
296: }
297: 
298: /// A node in the executable DAG.
299: #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
300: pub struct ExecNode {
301:     pub id: String,
302:     pub module_name: String,
303:     pub input: serde_json::Value,
304:     pub retry: Option<RetryConfig>,
305:     pub timeout_ms: u64,
306:     pub state: ExecutionState,
307: }
308: 
309: /// A materialized executable DAG backed by petgraph.
310: pub struct ExecutableDag {
311:     pub graph: DiGraph<ExecNode, ()>,
312:     pub node_indices: HashMap<String, NodeIndex>,
313:     pub run_graph_id: String,
314: }
315: 
316: /// Result of a DAG execution with cross-step flow state.
317: #[derive(Debug, Clone, serde::Serialize)]
318: pub struct RunResult {
319:     pub run_graph_id: String,
320:     /// Per-module results keyed by module path (backward compat alias)
321:     pub results: HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>,
322:     /// Accumulated flow state — same data as `results` but named for clarity
323:     pub flow_state: HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>,
324:     pub completed_at: chrono::DateTime<chrono::Utc>,
325: }
326: 
327: #[cfg(test)]
328: mod tests {
329:     use super::*;
330: 
331:     #[test]
332:     fn test_resolve_state_refs_direct() {
333:         let mut state = HashMap::new();
334:         state.insert("step_a".to_string(), serde_json::json!({"value": 42}));
335: 
336:         let result = resolve_state_refs(&serde_json::json!("${step_a}"), &state);
337:         assert_eq!(result, serde_json::json!({"value": 42}));
338: 
339:         let result = resolve_state_refs(&serde_json::json!("${step_a.value}"), &state);
340:         assert_eq!(result, serde_json::json!(42));
341: 
342:         let result = resolve_state_refs(&serde_json::json!("hello"), &state);
343:         assert_eq!(result, serde_json::json!("hello"));
344: 
345:         let result = resolve_state_refs(&serde_json::json!("${missing}"), &state);
346:         assert_eq!(result, serde_json::json!("${missing}"));
347:     }
348: 
349:     #[test]
350:     fn test_resolve_state_refs_nested_object() {
351:         let mut state = HashMap::new();
352:         state.insert("step_b".to_string(), serde_json::json!({"msg": "OK"}));
353: 
354:         let input = serde_json::json!({
355:             "data": "${step_b}",
356:             "fallback": "static_value",
357:         });
358:         let result = resolve_state_refs(&input, &state);
359:         assert_eq!(result["data"], serde_json::json!({"msg": "OK"}));
360:         assert_eq!(result["fallback"], "static_value");
361:     }
362: 
363:     #[test]
364:     fn test_resolve_state_refs_array() {
365:         let mut state = HashMap::new();
366:         state.insert("step_c".to_string(), serde_json::json!("result_c"));
367: 
368:         let input = serde_json::json!(["${step_c}", "literal", 123]);
369:         let result = resolve_state_refs(&input, &state);
370:         assert_eq!(result[0], "result_c");
371:         assert_eq!(result[1], "literal");
372:         assert_eq!(result[2], 123);
373:     }
374: 
375:     #[test]
376:     fn test_resolve_state_refs_missing_field() {
377:         let mut state = HashMap::new();
378:         state.insert("x".to_string(), serde_json::json!({"a": 1}));
379: 
380:         let result = resolve_state_refs(&serde_json::json!("${x.missing}"), &state);
381:         assert_eq!(result, serde_json::Value::Null);
382:     }
383: }

1: //! Flow DAG engine for Automaton.
2: //! Handles branch_one, branch_all, forloop, whileloop, failure_module execution.
3: //! When executed via the Runtime, modules are spawned as subprocesses with proper
4: //! state management and cross-step reference resolution.
5: 
6: use std::collections::HashMap;
7: use std::sync::Arc;
8: 
9: use automaton_core::backend::RegistryBackend;
10: use automaton_core::*;
11: use automaton_runtime::Runtime;
12: 
13: pub struct FlowEngine;
14: 
15: impl FlowEngine {
16:     /// Flatten a flow definition into an ordered list of executable steps
17:     /// with dependency resolution, handling branches and loops.
18:     pub fn flatten(flow: &FlowDefinition) -> Result<Vec<ExecStep>> {
19:         let mut steps = Vec::new();
20:         Self::flatten_steps(&flow.steps, &flow.default_retry, flow.default_timeout_ms, &mut steps, 0)?;
21:         Ok(steps)
22:     }
23: 
24:     fn flatten_steps(
25:         input: &[FlowStep],
26:         default_retry: &Option<RetryConfig>,
27:         default_timeout: u64,
28:         result: &mut Vec<ExecStep>,
29:         depth: usize,
30:     ) -> Result<()> {
31:         if depth > 10 {
32:             return Err(AutomatonError::Other("Flow nesting too deep (>10)".into()));
33:         }
34:         for step in input {
35:             let retry = step.retry.clone().or_else(|| default_retry.clone());
36:             match &step.kind {
37:                 FlowStepKind::Script => {
38:                     result.push(ExecStep {
39:                         id: step.id.clone(),
40:                         kind: ExecStepKind::Script,
41:                         script_path: step.script_path.clone(),
42:                         input: step.input.clone(),
43:                         retry,
44:                         timeout_ms: step.timeout_ms.max(default_timeout),
45:                         depends_on: step.depends_on.clone(),
46:                         sleep_after_ms: step.sleep_after_ms,
47:                         stop_if: step.stop_if.clone(),
48:                         failure_step: step.failure_step.clone(),
49:                     });
50:                 }
51:                 FlowStepKind::Shell { command, shell } => {
52:                     result.push(ExecStep {
53:                         id: step.id.clone(),
54:                         kind: ExecStepKind::Shell { command: command.clone(), shell: shell.clone() },
55:                         script_path: step.script_path.clone(),
56:                         input: step.input.clone(),
57:                         retry,
58:                         timeout_ms: step.timeout_ms.max(default_timeout),
59:                         depends_on: step.depends_on.clone(),
60:                         sleep_after_ms: step.sleep_after_ms,
61:                         stop_if: step.stop_if.clone(),
62:                         failure_step: step.failure_step.clone(),
63:                     });
64:                 }
65:                 FlowStepKind::Sleep => {
66:                     result.push(ExecStep {
67:                         id: step.id.clone(),
68:                         kind: ExecStepKind::Sleep,
69:                         script_path: None,
70:                         input: serde_json::json!({"duration_ms": step.sleep_after_ms.unwrap_or(1000)}),
71:                         retry: None,
72:                         timeout_ms: step.timeout_ms.max(default_timeout),
73:                         depends_on: step.depends_on.clone(),
74:                         sleep_after_ms: step.sleep_after_ms,
75:                         stop_if: None,
76:                         failure_step: step.failure_step.clone(),
77:                     });
78:                 }
79:                 FlowStepKind::BranchOne(branches) => {
80:                     let branch_steps: Vec<Vec<String>> = branches.iter().map(|b| {
81:                         let mut ids = Vec::new();
82:                         for bs in b {
83:                             ids.push(bs.id.clone());
84:                         }
85:                         ids
86:                     }).collect();
87:                     result.push(ExecStep {
88:                         id: format!("{}__branch_one", step.id),
89:                         kind: ExecStepKind::BranchOne(branch_steps),
90:                         script_path: None,
91:                         input: step.input.clone(),
92:                         retry: None,
93:                         timeout_ms: step.timeout_ms.max(default_timeout),
94:                         depends_on: step.depends_on.clone(),
95:                         sleep_after_ms: None,
96:                         stop_if: None,
97:                         failure_step: None,
98:                     });
99:                     for branch in branches {
100:                         Self::flatten_steps(branch, default_retry, default_timeout, result, depth + 1)?;
101:                     }
102:                 }
103:                 FlowStepKind::BranchAll(branches) => {
104:                     let branch_steps: Vec<Vec<String>> = branches.iter().map(|b| {
105:                         b.iter().map(|bs| bs.id.clone()).collect()
106:                     }).collect();
107:                     result.push(ExecStep {
108:                         id: format!("{}__branch_all", step.id),
109:                         kind: ExecStepKind::BranchAll(branch_steps),
110:                         script_path: None,
111:                         input: step.input.clone(),
112:                         retry: None,
113:                         timeout_ms: step.timeout_ms.max(default_timeout),
114:                         depends_on: step.depends_on.clone(),
115:                         sleep_after_ms: None,
116:                         stop_if: None,
117:                         failure_step: None,
118:                     });
119:                     for branch in branches {
120:                         Self::flatten_steps(branch, default_retry, default_timeout, result, depth + 1)?;
121:                     }
122:                 }
123:                 FlowStepKind::ForLoop { iterator, steps } => {
124:                     let body_ids: Vec<String> = steps.iter().map(|s| s.id.clone()).collect();
125:                     result.push(ExecStep {
126:                         id: format!("{}__forloop", step.id),
127:                         kind: ExecStepKind::ForLoop {
128:                             iterator: iterator.clone(),
129:                             body_ids: body_ids.clone(),
130:                             body_steps: steps.clone(),
131:                         },
132:                         script_path: None,
133:                         input: step.input.clone(),
134:                         retry: None,
135:                         timeout_ms: step.timeout_ms.max(default_timeout),
136:                         depends_on: step.depends_on.clone(),
137:                         sleep_after_ms: None,
138:                         stop_if: None,
139:                         failure_step: None,
140:                     });
141:                     // Flatten body steps for dependency resolution
142:                     Self::flatten_steps(steps, default_retry, default_timeout, result, depth + 1)?;
143:                 }
144:                 FlowStepKind::WhileLoop { condition, steps, max_iterations } => {
145:                     let body_ids: Vec<String> = steps.iter().map(|s| s.id.clone()).collect();
146:                     result.push(ExecStep {
147:                         id: format!("{}__whileloop", step.id),
148:                         kind: ExecStepKind::WhileLoop {
149:                             condition: condition.clone(),
150:                             max_iterations: *max_iterations,
151:                             body_ids: body_ids.clone(),
152:                             body_steps: steps.clone(),
153:                         },
154:                         script_path: None,
155:                         input: step.input.clone(),
156:                         retry: None,
157:                         timeout_ms: step.timeout_ms.max(default_timeout),
158:                         depends_on: step.depends_on.clone(),
159:                         sleep_after_ms: None,
160:                         stop_if: None,
161:                         failure_step: None,
162:                     });
163:                     Self::flatten_steps(steps, default_retry, default_timeout, result, depth + 1)?;
164:                 }
165:                 FlowStepKind::CallFlow { flow_path, input } => {
166:                     result.push(ExecStep {
167:                         id: step.id.clone(),
168:                         kind: ExecStepKind::CallFlow { flow_path: flow_path.clone(), input: input.clone() },
169:                         script_path: step.script_path.clone(),
170:                         input: step.input.clone(),
171:                         retry: None,
172:                         timeout_ms: step.timeout_ms.max(default_timeout),
173:                         depends_on: step.depends_on.clone(),
174:                         sleep_after_ms: None,
175:                         stop_if: None,
176:                         failure_step: None,
177:                     });
178:                 }
179:                 FlowStepKind::FailureModule => {
180:                     result.push(ExecStep {
181:                         id: step.id.clone(),
182:                         kind: ExecStepKind::FailureModule,
183:                         script_path: step.script_path.clone(),
184:                         input: step.input.clone(),
185:                         retry: None,
186:                         timeout_ms: step.timeout_ms.max(default_timeout),
187:                         depends_on: step.depends_on.clone(),
188:                         sleep_after_ms: None,
189:                         stop_if: None,
190:                         failure_step: None,
191:                     });
192:                 }
193:             }
194:         }
195:         Ok(())
196:     }
197: 
198:     /// Execute a flow using the Runtime for actual module subprocess execution.
199:     /// Resolves `$var:/$res:` references via the Registry, evaluates loops,
200:     /// and manages cross-step flow state.
201:     /// This is the backward-compatible 4-param version (failure_handler = None).
202:     pub async fn execute(
203:         steps: &[ExecStep],
204:         backend: Option<&Arc<dyn RegistryBackend>>,
205:         runtime: &Runtime,
206:         build_cache_dir: &std::path::Path,
207:     ) -> Result<Vec<(String, serde_json::Value)>> {
208:         Self::execute_with_handlers(steps, backend, runtime, build_cache_dir, None).await
209:     }
210: 
211:     /// Like `execute()` but with an optional failure_handler module path.
212:     /// When a step fails and `failure_handler` is set (and the step has no
213:     /// per-step `failure_step`), a synthetic FailureModule step is recorded
214:     /// and execution continues instead of erroring out.
215:     ///
216:     /// This also executes all independent (dependency-met) steps concurrently
217:     /// via `futures::future::join_all`.
218:     pub async fn execute_with_handlers(
219:         steps: &[ExecStep],
220:         backend: Option<&Arc<dyn RegistryBackend>>,
221:         runtime: &Runtime,
222:         build_cache_dir: &std::path::Path,
223:         failure_handler: Option<&str>,
224:     ) -> Result<Vec<(String, serde_json::Value)>> {
225:         let mut results: HashMap<String, serde_json::Value> = HashMap::new();
226:         let mut completed = std::collections::HashSet::<String>::new();
227:         let mut pending: std::collections::VecDeque<usize> = (0..steps.len()).collect();
228:         let mut max_rounds = 1000;
229:         // Track completion order for deterministic output
230:         let mut completion_order: Vec<String> = Vec::new();
231: 
232:         while !pending.is_empty() && max_rounds > 0 {
233:             max_rounds -= 1;
234: 
235:             // Separate ready steps from not-ready steps
236:             let mut ready = Vec::new();
237:             let mut not_ready = Vec::new();
238:             while let Some(idx) = pending.pop_front() {
239:                 if steps[idx].depends_on.iter().all(|d| completed.contains(d)) {
240:                     ready.push(idx);
241:                 } else {
242:                     not_ready.push(idx);
243:                 }
244:             }
245:             pending.extend(not_ready);
246: 
247:             if ready.is_empty() {
248:                 // All remaining steps have unmet deps — deadlock
249:                 break;
250:             }
251: 
252:             // Pre-resolve $var:/$res: references for all ready steps
253:             let mut resolved_inputs = Vec::with_capacity(ready.len());
254:             for &idx in &ready {
255:                 let step = &steps[idx];
256:                 let ri = if let Some(be) = &backend {
257:                     be.resolve_references(&step.input).await
258:                         .unwrap_or_else(|_| step.input.clone())
259:                 } else {
260:                     step.input.clone()
261:                 };
262:                 resolved_inputs.push(ri);
263:             }
264: 
265:             // Build futures for all ready steps, handling stop_if inline
266:             let mut futs = Vec::with_capacity(ready.len());
267:             let mut skip_results: Vec<(String, serde_json::Value)> = Vec::new();
268: 
269:             for (&idx, ri) in ready.iter().zip(resolved_inputs.iter()) {
270:                 let step = &steps[idx];
271: 
272:                 // Check stop_if before dispatching
273:                 let should_skip = step.stop_if.as_ref()
274:                     .map(|cond| evaluate_condition(cond, &results))
275:                     .unwrap_or(false);
276:                 if should_skip {
277:                     let reason = step.stop_if.as_deref().unwrap_or("stop_if_triggered");
278:                     skip_results.push((
279:                         step.id.clone(),
280:                         serde_json::json!({"status": "skipped", "reason": reason}),
281:                     ));
282:                     continue;
283:                 }
284: 
285:                 // Resolve cross-step state references
286:                 let resolved_input = crate::resolve_state_refs(ri, &results);
287: 
288:                 // Clone data for the async future (avoid borrow conflicts)
289:                 let step_clone = step.clone();
290:                 let steps_vec = steps.to_vec();
291:                 let bcd = build_cache_dir.to_path_buf();
292:                 let be_clone = backend.cloned();
293:                 let results_snapshot = results.clone();
294:                 let step_id = step.id.clone();
295: 
296:                 futs.push(async move {
297:                     let outcome = Self::execute_step_inner(
298:                         &step_clone,
299:                         &resolved_input,
300:                         &results_snapshot,
301:                         runtime,
302:                         &bcd,
303:                         be_clone.as_ref(),
304:                         &steps_vec,
305:                     ).await;
306:                     (step_id, outcome)
307:                 });
308:             }
309: 
310:             // Process skipped results immediately
311:             for (id, val) in skip_results {
312:                 completed.insert(id.clone());
313:                 completion_order.push(id.clone());
314:                 results.insert(id, val);
315:             }
316: 
317:             // Execute all ready steps concurrently
318:             let outcomes: Vec<(String, std::result::Result<serde_json::Value, AutomatonError>)> =
319:                 futures::future::join_all(futs).await;
320: 
321:             for (step_id, outcome) in outcomes {
322:                 match outcome {
323:                     Ok(output) => {
324:                         completed.insert(step_id.clone());
325:                         completion_order.push(step_id.clone());
326:                         results.insert(step_id, output);
327:                     }
328:                     Err(e) => {
329:                         // Check per-step failure_step first
330:                         let step_info = steps.iter().find(|s| s.id == step_id).ok_or_else(||
331:                             AutomatonError::Other(format!("Step '{step_id}' not found after execution"))
332:                         )?;
333:                         if let Some(fallback) = &step_info.failure_step {
334:                             completed.insert(step_id.clone());
335:                             completion_order.push(step_id.clone());
336:                             results.insert(step_id, serde_json::json!({
337:                                 "error": e.to_string(),
338:                                 "fallback": fallback,
339:                             }));
340:                         } else if let Some(handler) = failure_handler {
341:                             // Use the on_failure handler — record a synthetic FailureModule
342:                             let handler_id = "__on_failure__";
343:                             completed.insert(step_id.clone());
344:                             completion_order.push(step_id.clone());
345:                             results.insert(step_id.clone(), serde_json::json!({
346:                                 "error": e.to_string(),
347:                                 "handler_triggered": handler,
348:                             }));
349:                             completed.insert(handler_id.to_string());
350:                             completion_order.push(handler_id.to_string());
351:                             results.insert(handler_id.to_string(), serde_json::json!({
352:                                 "status": "failure_handler_ready",
353:                                 "handled_error": e.to_string(),
354:                                 "for_step": step_id,
355:                                 "handler": handler,
356:                             }));
357:                         } else {
358:                             return Err(e);
359:                         }
360:                     }
361:                 }
362:             }
363:         }
364: 
365:         // Return results in completion order (HashMap has undefined iteration order)
366:         let ordered: Vec<(String, serde_json::Value)> = completion_order.iter()
367:             .filter_map(|id| results.remove(id).map(|v| (id.clone(), v)))
368:             .collect();
369:         Ok(ordered)
370:     }
371: 
372:     /// Execute a SINGLE step with the given resolved input and state snapshot.
373:     /// Returns the output value for this step.
374:     async fn execute_step_inner(
375:         step: &ExecStep,
376:         resolved_input: &serde_json::Value,
377:         results_snapshot: &HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>,
378:         runtime: &Runtime,
379:         build_cache_dir: &std::path::Path,
380:         backend: Option<&Arc<dyn RegistryBackend>>,
381:         steps: &[ExecStep],
382:     ) -> Result<serde_json::Value> {
383:         match &step.kind {
384:             ExecStepKind::Script => {
385:                 let script_path = step.script_path.as_deref().unwrap_or("");
386:                 let binary_path = build_cache_dir.join(script_path.replace('.', "_"));
387:                 if binary_path.exists() {
388:                     if let Some(retry_cfg) = &step.retry {
389:                         runtime.run_with_retry(&binary_path, resolved_input, retry_cfg, step.timeout_ms).await
390:                     } else {
391:                         runtime.run_binary(&binary_path, resolved_input, step.timeout_ms).await
392:                     }
393:                 } else {
394:                     Ok(serde_json::json!({"status": "no_binary_found", "path": script_path}))
395:                 }
396:             }
397:             ExecStepKind::Shell { command, shell } => {
398:                 let shell_bin = shell.as_deref().unwrap_or("sh");
399: 
400:                 // Shell execution helper with kill_on_drop for orphan cleanup
401:                 let run_shell = || async {
402:                     let timeout = std::time::Duration::from_millis(step.timeout_ms);
403:                     let child = tokio::process::Command::new(shell_bin)
404:                         .arg("-c")
405:                         .arg(command.as_str())
406:                         .kill_on_drop(true)
407:                         .stdout(std::process::Stdio::piped())
408:                         .stderr(std::process::Stdio::piped())
409:                         .spawn()
410:                         .map_err(|e| AutomatonError::ExecutionFailed(format!("Failed to spawn shell: {e}")))?;
411: 
412:                     match tokio::time::timeout(timeout, child.wait_with_output()).await {
413:                         Ok(Ok(output)) => {
414:                             if output.status.success() {
415:                                 Ok(serde_json::json!({
416:                                     "stdout": String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).to_string(),
417:                                     "stderr": String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr).to_string(),
418:                                     "exit_code": output.status.code().unwrap_or(0),
419:                                 }))
420:                             } else {
421:                                 let stderr = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr);
422:                                 let stdout = String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout);
423:                                 Err(AutomatonError::ExecutionFailed(format!(
424:                                     "Shell command failed (exit {}): {}",
425:                                     output.status.code().unwrap_or(-1),
426:                                     if stderr.is_empty() { &stdout } else { &stderr },
427:                                 )))
428:                             }
429:                         }
430:                         Ok(Err(e)) => Err(AutomatonError::ExecutionFailed(format!("Shell process error: {e}"))),
431:                         Err(_) => Err(AutomatonError::Timeout(step.timeout_ms)),
432:                     }
433:                 };
434: 
435:                 // Run with optional retry
436:                 if let Some(retry_cfg) = &step.retry {
437:                     let mut last_error = String::new();
438:                     let mut delay = retry_cfg.delay_ms;
439:                     let mut output = None;
440:                     for attempt in 1..=retry_cfg.max_attempts {
441:                         match run_shell().await {
442:                             Ok(out) => { output = Some(out); break; }
443:                             Err(e) => {
444:                                 last_error = e.to_string();
445:                                 if attempt < retry_cfg.max_attempts {
446:                                     if delay > 0 {
447:                                         tokio::time::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(delay)).await;
448:                                     }
449:                                     delay = match retry_cfg.backoff {
450:                                         automaton_core::BackoffKind::Fixed => retry_cfg.delay_ms,
451:                                         automaton_core::BackoffKind::Linear => retry_cfg.delay_ms * (attempt as u64 + 1),
452:                                         automaton_core::BackoffKind::Exponential => retry_cfg.delay_ms * (1u64 << attempt),
453:                                     };
454:                                 }
455:                             }
456:                         }
457:                     }
458:                     output.ok_or_else(|| AutomatonError::ExecutionFailed(
459:                         format!("All {} attempts failed. Last error: {last_error}", retry_cfg.max_attempts)
460:                     ))
461:                 } else {
462:                     run_shell().await
463:                 }
464:             }
465:             ExecStepKind::Sleep => {
466:                 let ms = step.sleep_after_ms.unwrap_or(1000);
467:                 tokio::time::sleep(std::time::Duration::from_millis(ms)).await;
468:                 Ok(serde_json::json!({"slept_ms": ms}))
469:             }
470:             ExecStepKind::BranchOne(branches) => {
471:                     let mut branch_result = None;
472:                 for branch_ids in branches {
473:                     for bid in branch_ids {
474:                         if let Some(bstep) = steps.iter().find(|s| s.id == *bid) {
475:                             let script_path = bstep.script_path.as_deref().unwrap_or("");
476:                             let bp = build_cache_dir.join(script_path.replace('.', "_"));
477:                             if bp.exists() {
478:                                 match runtime.run_binary(&bp, resolved_input, bstep.timeout_ms).await {
479:                                     Ok(r) => { branch_result = Some(r); break; }
480:                                     Err(_) => continue,
481:                                 }
482:                             } else if let ExecStepKind::Shell { command, shell } = &bstep.kind {
483:                                 // Shell-based fallback: first branch that succeeds
484:                                 let shell_bin = shell.as_deref().unwrap_or("sh");
485:                                 let child = tokio::process::Command::new(shell_bin)
486:                                     .arg("-c")
487:                                     .arg(command.as_str())
488:                                     .kill_on_drop(true)
489:                                     .stdout(std::process::Stdio::piped())
490:                                     .stderr(std::process::Stdio::piped())
491:                                     .spawn()
492:                                     .map_err(|e| AutomatonError::ExecutionFailed(format!("BranchOne shell: {e}")))?;
493:                                 let to = std::time::Duration::from_millis(bstep.timeout_ms.max(step.timeout_ms));
494:                                 match tokio::time::timeout(to, child.wait_with_output()).await {
495:                                     Ok(Ok(output)) if output.status.success() => {
496:                                         branch_result = Some(serde_json::json!({
497:                                             "stdout": String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).to_string(),
498:                                             "stderr": String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr).to_string(),
499:                                             "exit_code": output.status.code().unwrap_or(0),
500:                                         }));
501:                                         break;
502:                                     }
503:                                     _ => continue,
504:                                 }
505:                             }
506:                         }
507:                     }
508:                     if branch_result.is_some() { break; }
509:                 }
510:                 Ok(branch_result.unwrap_or(serde_json::json!({"error": "all_branches_failed"})))
511:             }
512:             ExecStepKind::BranchAll(branches) => {
513:                 let mut all_results = Vec::new();
514:                 for branch_ids in branches {
515:                     for bid in branch_ids {
516:                         if let Some(bstep) = steps.iter().find(|s| s.id == *bid) {
517:                             let script_path = bstep.script_path.as_deref().unwrap_or("");
518:                             let bp = build_cache_dir.join(script_path.replace('.', "_"));
519:                             if bp.exists() {
520:                                 match runtime.run_binary(&bp, resolved_input, bstep.timeout_ms).await {
521:                                     Ok(r) => all_results.push(r),
522:                                     Err(e) => all_results.push(serde_json::json!({"error": e.to_string()})),
523:                                 }
524:                             } else if let ExecStepKind::Shell { command, shell } = &bstep.kind {
525:                                 // Shell-based fallback for all branches
526:                                 let shell_bin = shell.as_deref().unwrap_or("sh");
527:                                 let child = tokio::process::Command::new(shell_bin)
528:                                     .arg("-c")
529:                                     .arg(command.as_str())
530:                                     .kill_on_drop(true)
531:                                     .stdout(std::process::Stdio::piped())
532:                                     .stderr(std::process::Stdio::piped())
533:                                     .spawn()
534:                                     .map_err(|e| AutomatonError::ExecutionFailed(format!("BranchAll shell: {e}")))?;
535:                                 let to = std::time::Duration::from_millis(bstep.timeout_ms.max(step.timeout_ms));
536:                                 match tokio::time::timeout(to, child.wait_with_output()).await {
537:                                     Ok(Ok(output)) => {
538:                                         all_results.push(serde_json::json!({
539:                                             "stdout": String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stdout).to_string(),
540:                                             "stderr": String::from_utf8_lossy(&output.stderr).to_string(),
541:                                             "exit_code": output.status.code().unwrap_or(0),
542:                                         }));
543:                                     }
544:                                     Ok(Err(e)) => all_results.push(serde_json::json!({"error": e.to_string()})),
545:                                     Err(_) => all_results.push(serde_json::json!({"error": "timeout"})),

703: /// Resolve an iterator value from a ForLoop definition.
704: /// Checks the resolved_input for an array, then falls back to upstream flow state.
705: fn resolve_iterable(resolved_input: &serde_json::Value, upstream: Option<&serde_json::Value>) -> Vec<serde_json::Value> {
706:     // First check the flow input
707:     if let Some(arr) = resolved_input.as_array() {
708:         return arr.clone();
709:     }
710:     // Then check upstream state
711:     if let Some(up) = upstream {
712:         if let Some(arr) = up.as_array() {
713:             return arr.clone();
714:         }
715:         // If it's an object with an 'items' or 'results' field
716:         if let Some(items) = up.get("items").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {
717:             return items.clone();
718:         }
719:         if let Some(results) = up.get("results").and_then(|v| v.as_array()) {
720:             return results.clone();
721:         }
722:         // Wrap scalar in a single-element vec
723:         if !up.is_null() {
724:             return vec![up.clone()];
725:         }
726:     }
727:     // Default: single iteration with empty object
728:     vec![serde_json::json!({})]
729: }
730: 
731: /// Evaluate a WhileLoop condition expression against current flow state.
732: /// Supports simple expressions like `${step.status} == "completed"`.
733: fn evaluate_condition(condition: &str, results: &HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>) -> bool {
734:     // Simple evaluation: resolve state refs then check equality
735:     let cond = condition.trim();
736:     if cond.is_empty() {
737:         return false;
738:     }
739: 
740:     // If the condition is just a state reference like `${step.status}`,
741:     // check if it resolves to a truthy value
742:     if cond.starts_with("${") && cond.ends_with("}") && !cond.contains(' ') {
743:         let ref_val = crate::resolve_state_refs(&serde_json::json!(cond), results);
744:         return ref_val.is_object() || ref_val.is_array() || ref_val.as_bool() == Some(true)
745:             || ref_val.as_str().map(|s| s == "true" || s == "completed" || s == "ok").unwrap_or(false)
746:             || (ref_val.is_number() && ref_val.as_f64().map(|v| v > 0.0).unwrap_or(false));
747:     }
748: 
749:     // Simple string comparison: "${x.status} == completed"
750:     if let Some(eq_pos) = cond.find("==") {
751:         let left_str = cond[..eq_pos].trim();
752:         let right_str = cond[eq_pos + 2..].trim().trim_matches('"');
753:         let left_val = if left_str.starts_with("${") {
754:             crate::resolve_state_refs(&serde_json::json!(left_str), results)
755:         } else {
756:             serde_json::json!(left_str)
757:         };
758:         let left_display = left_val.as_str().map(|s| s.to_string())
759:             .or_else(|| left_val.as_f64().map(|n| n.to_string()))
760:             .unwrap_or_default();
761:         return left_display == right_str;
762:     }
763: 
764:     // Simple string inequality: "${x.count} > 0"
765:     if let Some(gt_pos) = cond.find('>') {
766:         let left_str = cond[..gt_pos].trim();
767:         let right_str = cond[gt_pos + 1..].trim();
768:         let left_val = if left_str.starts_with("${") {
769:             crate::resolve_state_refs(&serde_json::json!(left_str), results)
770:         } else {
771:             serde_json::json!(left_str)
772:         };
773:         let left_num = left_val.as_f64().unwrap_or(0.0);
774:         let right_num: f64 = right_str.parse().unwrap_or(0.0);
775:         return left_num > right_num;
776:     }
777: 
778:     // Default: resolve the condition as a state ref and check truthiness
779:     let resolved = crate::resolve_state_refs(&serde_json::json!(cond), results);
780:     resolved.as_bool() == Some(true)
781:         || resolved.as_str().map(|s| s == "true" || s == "1").unwrap_or(false)
782:         || resolved.as_f64().map(|v| v > 0.0).unwrap_or(false)
783: }
784: 
785: /// A step ready for execution after flattening
786: #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
787: pub struct ExecStep {
788:     pub id: String,
789:     pub kind: ExecStepKind,
790:     pub script_path: Option<String>,
791:     pub input: serde_json::Value,
792:     pub retry: Option<RetryConfig>,
793:     pub timeout_ms: u64,
794:     pub depends_on: Vec<String>,
795:     pub sleep_after_ms: Option<u64>,
796:     pub stop_if: Option<String>,
797:     pub failure_step: Option<String>,
798: }
799: 
800: #[derive(Debug, Clone)]
801: pub enum ExecStepKind {
802:     Script,
803:     Shell {
804:         command: String,
805:         shell: Option<String>,
806:     },
807:     Sleep,
808:     BranchOne(Vec<Vec<String>>),
809:     BranchAll(Vec<Vec<String>>),
810:     ForLoop {
811:         iterator: String,
812:         body_ids: Vec<String>,
813:         body_steps: Vec<FlowStep>,
814:     },
815:     WhileLoop {
816:         condition: String,
817:         max_iterations: u32,
818:         body_ids: Vec<String>,
819:         body_steps: Vec<FlowStep>,
820:     },
821:     FailureModule,
822:     CallFlow {
823:         flow_path: String,
824:         input: Option<serde_json::Value>,
825:     },
826: }
827: 
828: #[cfg(test)]
829: mod tests {
830:     use super::*;
831: 
832:     #[test]
833:     fn test_evaluate_condition_truthy_state_ref() {
834:         let mut results = HashMap::new();
835:         results.insert("step_a".into(), serde_json::json!({"status": "completed"}));
836:         assert!(evaluate_condition("${step_a.status}", &results));
837:     }
838: 
839:     #[test]
840:     fn test_evaluate_condition_eq_comparison() {
841:         let mut results = HashMap::new();
842:         results.insert("step_a".into(), serde_json::json!({"status": "completed"}));
843:         assert!(evaluate_condition(r#"${step_a.status} == "completed""#, &results));
844:         assert!(!evaluate_condition(r#"${step_a.status} == "failed""#, &results));
845:     }
846: 
847:     #[test]
848:     fn test_evaluate_condition_gt_comparison() {
849:         let mut results = HashMap::new();
850:         results.insert("step_a".into(), serde_json::json!({"count": 5}));
851:         assert!(evaluate_condition("${step_a.count} > 0", &results));
852:         assert!(!evaluate_condition("${step_a.count} > 10", &results));
853:     }
854: 
855:     #[test]
856:     fn test_evaluate_condition_empty() {
857:         assert!(!evaluate_condition("", &HashMap::new()));
858:     }
859: 
860:     #[test]
861:     fn test_resolve_iterable_from_input() {
862:         let input = serde_json::json!([1, 2, 3]);
863:         let result = resolve_iterable(&input, None);
864:         assert_eq!(result.len(), 3);
865:     }

869:         let upstream = serde_json::json!([4, 5, 6]);
870:         let result = resolve_iterable(&serde_json::json!({}), Some(&upstream));

182:         with_db(&self.db, |db| {
183:             let stmt = db.prepare(
184:                 "SELECT id, source, target, kind, properties, created_at FROM edges WHERE target = ?1 ORDER BY created_at",
185:             )?;
186:             map_edges_query(stmt, rusqlite::params![node_id])
187:         })
188:     }
189: 
190:     pub fn get_dependency_chain(&self, start_id: &str) -> Result<Vec<Node>> {
191:         let mut visited: HashSet<String> = HashSet::new();
192:         let mut queue = std::collections::VecDeque::new();
193:         let mut result = vec![];
194:         queue.push_back(start_id.to_string());
195:         while let Some(current) = queue.pop_front() {
196:             if !visited.insert(current.clone()) {
197:                 continue;
198:             }
199:             if let Some(node) = self.get_node(&current)? {
200:                 result.push(node);
201:             }
202:             let edges = self.get_outgoing_edges(&current)?;
203:             for edge in edges {
204:                 if matches!(edge.kind, EdgeKind::DependsOn) {
205:                     queue.push_back(edge.target);
206:                 }
207:             }
208:         }
209:         Ok(result)
210:     }
211: 
212:     pub fn find_path(&self, from: &str, to: &str) -> Result<Vec<Vec<NodeAndEdge>>> {
213:         let all_edges = self.all_edges()?;
214:         let mut adj: HashMap<String, Vec<(String, EdgeKind)>> = HashMap::new();
215:         for edge in &all_edges {
216:             adj.entry(edge.source.clone())
217:                 .or_default()
218:                 .push((edge.target.clone(), edge.kind.clone()));
219:         }
220: 
221:         type StackEntry = (String, Vec<(String, EdgeKind)>, HashSet<String>);
222:         let mut paths: Vec<Vec<(String, EdgeKind)>> = vec![];
223:         let mut stack: Vec<StackEntry> = vec![(from.to_string(), vec![], HashSet::new())];
224: 
225:         while let Some((node, path_so_far, visited)) = stack.pop() {
226:             let mut visited = visited;
227:             if node == to {
228:                 paths.push(path_so_far.clone());
229:                 continue;
230:             }
231:             if !visited.insert(node.clone()) {
232:                 continue;
233:             }
234:             if path_so_far.len() > 10 {
235:                 continue;
236:             }
237:             if let Some(neighbors) = adj.get(&node) {
238:                 for (next, kind) in neighbors {
239:                     let mut new_path = path_so_far.clone();
240:                     new_path.push((next.clone(), kind.clone()));
241:                     stack.push((next.clone(), new_path, visited.clone()));
242:                 }
243:             }
244:         }
245: 
246:         let mut result = vec![];
247:         for raw_path in &paths {
248:             let mut segment = vec![];
249:             // Add the start node (not included in DFS path traversal)
250:             if let Some(node) = self.get_node(from)? {
251:                 segment.push(NodeAndEdge {
252:                     node,
253:                     edge_kind: raw_path.first().map(|(_, ek)| ek.clone()).unwrap_or(EdgeKind::DependsOn),
254:                 });
255:             }
256:             for (node_id, edge_kind) in raw_path {
257:                 if let Some(node) = self.get_node(node_id)? {
258:                     segment.push(NodeAndEdge {
259:                         node,
260:                         edge_kind: edge_kind.clone(),
261:                     });
262:                 }
263:             }
264:             result.push(segment);
265:         }
266:         Ok(result)
267:     }

273:             )?;

275:         })

279:         with_db(&self.db, |db| {
280:             let mut stmt = db.prepare(
281:                 "SELECT id, kind, name, properties, created_at FROM nodes ORDER BY created_at",
282:             )?;

292:     pub fn all_nodes_paginated(&self, limit: Option<u32>, offset: Option<u32>) -> Result<Vec<Node>> {
293:         with_db(&self.db, |db| {
294:             let mut sql = String::from(
295:                 "SELECT id, kind, name, properties, created_at FROM nodes ORDER BY created_at",
296:             );
297:             let mut params: Vec<Box<dyn rusqlite::types::ToSql>> = vec![];
298:             if let Some(l) = limit {
299:                 sql.push_str(" LIMIT ?");
300:                 params.push(Box::new(l as i64));
301:             }
302:             if let Some(o) = offset {
303:                 sql.push_str(" OFFSET ?");
304:                 params.push(Box::new(o as i64));
305:             }
306:             let mut stmt = db.prepare(&sql)?;
307:             let param_refs: Vec<&dyn rusqlite::types::ToSql> =
308:                 params.iter().map(|p| p.as_ref()).collect();
309:             let rows = stmt.query_map(param_refs.as_slice(), row_to_node)?;
310:             let mut result = vec![];
311:             for row in rows {
312:                 result.push(row?);
313:             }
314:             Ok(result)
315:         })
316:     }

318:     /// Find all nodes whose properties match all the given key-value pairs.
319:     /// Since SQLite stores properties as JSON text, this iterates all nodes
320:     /// and performs in-memory filtering — suitable for moderate-sized graphs.
321:     pub fn find_nodes_by_properties(
322:         &self,
323:         properties: &HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>,
324:     ) -> Result<Vec<NodeAndEdge>> {
325:         with_db(&self.db, |conn| {
326:             let mut stmt = conn.prepare(
327:                 "SELECT id, kind, name, properties, created_at FROM nodes ORDER BY created_at",
328:             )?;
329:             let rows = stmt.query_map([], row_to_node)?;
330:             let mut result = vec![];
331:             for row in rows {
332:                 let node = row?;
333:                 if properties
334:                     .iter()
335:                     .all(|(k, v)| node.properties.get(k) == Some(v))
336:                 {
337:                     result.push(NodeAndEdge {
338:                         node,
339:                         edge_kind: EdgeKind::DependsOn,
340:                     });
341:                 }
342:             }
343:             Ok(result)
344:         })
345:     }
346: 
347:     /// Paginated variant of `find_nodes_by_properties`.
348:     /// Filters all nodes by properties, then applies limit/offset.
349:     pub fn find_nodes_by_properties_paginated(
350:         &self,
351:         properties: &HashMap<String, serde_json::Value>,
352:         limit: usize,
353:         offset: usize,
354:     ) -> Result<Vec<NodeAndEdge>> {
355:         let all = self.find_nodes_by_properties(properties)?;
356:         let result: Vec<NodeAndEdge> = all.into_iter().skip(offset).take(limit).collect();
357:         Ok(result)
358:     }

378:     /// Get aggregated summary statistics about the graph.
379:     pub fn summarize(&self) -> Result<GraphSummary> {
380:         with_db(&self.db, |db| {
381:             let total_nodes: u64 = db.query_row("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM nodes", [], |row| {
382:                 row.get(0)
383:             })?;
384:             let total_edges: u64 = db.query_row("SELECT COUNT(*) FROM edges", [], |row| {
385:                 row.get(0)
386:             })?;
387: 
388:             let mut nodes_by_kind: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();
389:             let mut stmt = db.prepare("SELECT kind, COUNT(*) as cnt FROM nodes GROUP BY kind")?;
390:             let rows = stmt.query_map([], |row| {
391:                 let kind: String = row.get(0)?;
392:                 let cnt: u64 = row.get(1)?;
393:                 Ok((kind.trim_matches('"').to_string(), cnt))
394:             })?;
395:             for row in rows {
396:                 let (kind, cnt) = row?;
397:                 nodes_by_kind.insert(kind, cnt);
398:             }
399: 
400:             let mut edges_by_kind: HashMap<String, u64> = HashMap::new();
401:             let mut stmt = db.prepare("SELECT kind, COUNT(*) as cnt FROM edges GROUP BY kind")?;
402:             let rows = stmt.query_map([], |row| {
403:                 let kind: String = row.get(0)?;
404:                 let cnt: u64 = row.get(1)?;
405:                 Ok((kind.trim_matches('"').to_string(), cnt))
406:             })?;
407:             for row in rows {
408:                 let (kind, cnt) = row?;
409:                 edges_by_kind.insert(kind, cnt);
410:             }
411: 
412:             Ok(GraphSummary {
413:                 total_nodes,
414:                 total_edges,
415:                 nodes_by_kind,
416:                 edges_by_kind,
417:             })
418:         })
419:     }
420: }

422: /// Summary statistics about the knowledge graph.
423: #[derive(Debug, Clone, serde::Serialize)]
424: pub struct GraphSummary {
425:     pub total_nodes: u64,
426:     pub total_edges: u64,
427:     pub nodes_by_kind: HashMap<String, u64>,
428:     pub edges_by_kind: HashMap<String, u64>,
429: }

431: fn row_to_node(row: &rusqlite::Row) -> rusqlite::Result<Node> {
432:     let props_str: String = row.get(3)?;
433:     Ok(Node {
434:         id: row.get(0)?,
435:         kind: serde_json::from_str(&row.get::<_, String>(1)?).unwrap_or(NodeKind::Module),
436:         name: row.get(2)?,
437:         properties: serde_json::from_str(&props_str).unwrap_or_default(),
438:         created_at: chrono::DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339(&row.get::<_, String>(4)?)
439:             .map(|dt| dt.to_utc())
440:             .unwrap_or_default(),
441:     })
442: }
443: 
444: fn map_edges_query(
445:     mut stmt: rusqlite::Statement,
446:     params: impl rusqlite::Params,
447: ) -> Result<Vec<Edge>> {
448:     let rows = stmt.query_map(params, |row| {
449:         let props_str: String = row.get(4)?;
450:         Ok(Edge {
451:             id: row.get(0)?,
452:             source: row.get(1)?,
453:             target: row.get(2)?,
454:             kind: serde_json::from_str(&row.get::<_, String>(3)?).unwrap_or(EdgeKind::DependsOn),
455:             properties: serde_json::from_str(&props_str).unwrap_or_default(),
456:             created_at: chrono::DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339(&row.get::<_, String>(5)?)
457:                 .map(|dt| dt.to_utc())
458:                 .unwrap_or_default(),
459:         })
460:     })?;
461:     let mut result = vec![];
462:     for row in rows {
463:         result.push(row?);
464:     }
465:     Ok(result)
466: }

472: }

155:     pub fn register(
156:         &self,
157:         path: &str,
158:         source: &str,
159:         manifest: &AutomationManifest,
160:     ) -> Result<ModuleId> {
161:         let hash = ContentHash::compute(source.as_bytes());
162:         let now = chrono::Utc::now();
163:         let manifest_json = serde_json::to_string(manifest)?;
164: 
165:         with_registry(&self.db, |db| {
166:             db.execute(
167:                 "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO modules (path, version, hash, source, manifest, built, created_at) VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3, ?4, ?5, 0, ?6)",
168:                 rusqlite::params![path, manifest.version, hash.as_str(), source, manifest_json, now.to_rfc3339()],
169:             )?;
170: 
171:             for dep in &manifest.depends_on {
172:                 db.execute(
173:                     "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO dependencies (module_path, depends_on, version_req) VALUES (?1, ?2, ?3)",
174:                     rusqlite::params![path, dep.name, dep.version_req],
175:                 )?;
176:             }
177:             Ok(())
178:         })?;
179: 
180:         Ok(ModuleId {
181:             path: path.to_string(),
182:             version: semver::Version::parse(&manifest.version)
183:                 .map_err(|e: semver::Error| AutomatonError::Other(e.to_string()))?,
184:             hash,
185:             created_at: now,
186:         })
187:     }
